intent: Give every Flutter/Dart app a launcher icon from one shared family (charcoal field + the app's own theme accent as a glyph) instead of the stock Flutter 'beach' icon, and make future apps inherit that style automatically.
scope: python_pkg/app_icons/ (new package: style, glyphs, app registry, renderer, preview, CLI)
Non-goal: the per-app repositories themselves (dufs-cloud, screen-locker, wake-alarm, diet-guard, todo) are committed separately
Non-goal: iOS/web/Windows/macOS targets - none are configured on these apps
changes: style.py defines the family once (canvas 1024, charcoal #1B1D21, stroke 72, 560px adaptive safe box); every app contributes only an accent colour and a glyph.

render.centre_offset rasterises a glyph alone, measures its ink bounding box with ImageMagick, and translates (never scales) the fragment so all icons share one optical centre - this is what makes a row of icons line up.
render.safe_box_overflow warns when a glyph exceeds the adaptive-icon safe box; it caught three real overflows (cloud 646px tall, shield, checklist) that a launcher mask would have clipped.

generate.py writes three layers per app (icon, adaptive foreground, Android 13 monochrome) as SVG plus 1024px PNG, then drives flutter_launcher_icons for the Android mipmaps and mipmap-anydpi-v26 descriptor.
apps.py registry points dufs_client at ~/dufs-cloud/app: ~/dufs_client was a stale, remote-less checkout with the same package name that has since been deleted.

verification.evidence: Your code has been rated at 10.00/10
39 passed; every app_icons module at 100% line and branch coverage. External tools (rsvg-convert, magick, dart) are mocked, so the suite needs none installed.

verification.command: PYTHONPATH=~/testsAndMisc python3 -m python_pkg.app_icons generate --android (all 5 apps) then adb install -r + launcher screenshot
verification.evidence: All five apps show their new icon in the phone app drawer; no stock icon remains. Each install reported plain 'Success' with no 'Uninstalling old version' line, so no app data was wiped.

risks: Editing style.py restyles every app at once - that is intended, but a careless change silently affects all five.
Glyph negative space below half the stroke weight merges into a blob at launcher size; the fork hit this and had to be redrawn as a filled fork-and-knife silhouette.
The generator writes into sibling repositories by absolute path, so a moved or renamed repo makes generate fail (or, worse, target a stale checkout - exactly what happened with dufs_client).
rollback: git rm -r python_pkg/app_icons and revert this evidence file; nothing else in testsAndMisc depends on the package.
Per app repository, git checkout the mipmap PNGs and pubspec.yaml, and delete assets/icon plus the generated drawable-*/mipmap-anydpi-v26 directories.
After rollback, rebuild and adb install -r each app and confirm the launcher shows the previous icon.
